sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
The patient reports a knee injury.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports pain in the right knee, which he sustained while skiing over the weekend. He describes a 'pop' sensation and immediate pain following the injury. He reports the knee is swollen and painful, with difficulty walking. He has been taking Advil and icing the knee intermittently. He reports some bruising on his shoulder, likely from the fall. He also reports a trip to Mount Saint Helen and Washington, where he learned that maple syrup production is more prominent in the fall than in the summer.
Findings
The patient's vitals are within normal limits: temperature 97.9°F, heart rate 64 bpm, respirations 16 breaths per minute, blood pressure 130/84 mmHg, and oxygen saturation 98%. A physical exam reveals edema and ecchymosis around the right knee. Range of motion is limited due to pain, with increased pain on flexion and extension. Palpation elicits tenderness. A superficial bruise is noted on the patient's left shoulder.
Assessment
The patient has a knee contusion with associated pain and swelling. He also reports a shoulder bruise.
Plan
medical_concepts: limited range of motion of right knee

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: The patient should rest the knee. Apply ice two to three times a day for twenty minutes. Take ibuprofen every six to eight hours as needed. We will order a cervical x-ray to rule out any other injuries. Follow up in four weeks if symptoms persist. A physical therapy referral will be considered if the patient does not improve.

medical_concepts: limited range of motion of right knee
